verdict_payload.notes: All four doctrinally-load-bearing terms preserved verbatim: prayatnāt (with effort), yatamānaḥ (striving — the active term, not 'attaining' as a passive), saṁśuddha-kilbiṣaḥ (purified of contamination), aneka-janma-saṁsiddhaḥ (perfected through many births). Shankara's 'gathering of tendencies across many births' and Ramanuja's 'accumulation of merit collected over lifetimes' and 'even though he had once gone astray' are both correctly attributed without conflation. The metaphysical scope of multi-life perfection is explicitly named as exceeding the operational analog. The kilbiṣa-purification is correctly mapped to the small-grained cognitive bias each shipped failure produces (rather than to literal sin/karmic-residue). The verse's parām gatim is preserved as the destination beyond the engineering-reachable rung. PASS.

verdict_payload.real_insight: The 43-year-old staff engineer's apparent effortlessness in the architecture review is the residue of decades of striving and failure-encountered, not the residue of decades of avoidance. Implication: distinguish two career paths that look identical on the resume — the one that compounds via striving-with-failure and the one that compounds via tenure-without-striving. The verse rules out the second.
verdict_payload.objection: The verse explicitly rules out 'years equal mastery.' The active term is yatamānaḥ — striving. Falsifiability: 'tenure without striving does not produce saṁsiddhi.' Counter-example: 'the principal engineer who has not written meaningful code in five years and reviews PRs from political position rather than from substrate' — exactly the failure mode the verse refuses. The kilbiṣa-purification is mapped to the cognitive-bias-each-failure-produces — not to abstract sin. PASS.

verdict_payload.actionable_predicate: When evaluating senior engineers for promotion to staff/principal/distinguished levels, weight 'striving-encountered-failure' heavily. Concrete signal: how many times in the prior five years has the engineer stayed with a hard problem past the discomfort point rather than externalising it? Use peer-pair interviews to surface this — colleagues who have been in the trenches with the candidate.
verdict_payload.example_decision: Two senior engineers up for staff promotion. Engineer A has fifteen years at the same company, calm presence, technically clean reviews. Engineer B has sixteen years across three companies, chaired four serious incidents, rolled back two of her own architectural decisions in public post-mortems. The verse-predicate ranks B above A — the kilbiṣa was encountered, not avoided.

verdict_payload.actionable_predicate: Promotion-rubric weights striving-encountered-failure over tenure-without-striving. Falsifiable via peer-pair interview signal.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: Shankara's gathering-of-tendencies (saṁskāra-accumulation) and Ramanuja's accumulation-of-merit (puṇya-accumulation) are correctly distinguished. The two readings emphasise different mechanisms (psychological-substrate vs ethical-merit) and the engineering layer cites both without conflation. parām gatim is preserved as the verse's destination, explicitly named as exceeding engineering reach. The chapter's 'even though he had once gone astray' (Ramanuja's emphasis on the fall being accommodated) is correctly preserved. PASS.
prayatnāt, yatamānaḥ, saṁśuddha-kilbiṣaḥ, aneka-janma-saṁsiddhaḥ, parām gatim all preserved. PASS.

verdict_payload.satire_attempt: Forty-Three-Year-Old Staff Engineer Casually Identifies Architectural Flaw in Review, Single-Handedly Justifies Eighteen Years of Therapy. New industry conference 'Saṁsiddhi Summit' charges $4,800 to teach the secret of looking effortless during architecture review (you simply have to have shipped failures since 2008).
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The satire only lands by ignoring the verse's explicit refusal of conference-shortcut: there is no shortcut. The years are not optional. The conference-substitute is exactly what the falsifiability disqualifies. The verse names a destination beyond what any career reaches; the operational rung was earned through the years of striving the conference cannot replicate. PASS.

verse_specific_anchors: aneka-janma-saṁsiddhaḥ — perfection through many births is the verse's specific scope; the analog scopes operationally to decade-of-compounding while explicitly preserving the multi-life metaphysics
the eighteen-year compounding-across-five-databases scenario specifically illustrates the saṁskāra accumulation Shankara describes
verdict_payload.verdict_reason: Tested against 6.43 (substrate reattachment, single discontinuity), 6.44 (saṁskāra current, single transition), 4.10 (purification through knowledge, not many-births). PASS — verse-specific.

top_candidates.reasoning: The eighteen-year-compounding-across-databases scenario, the kilbiṣa-as-cognitive-bias-from-shipped-failures, and the explicit naming of parām gatim as a destination beyond engineering reach all point to 6.45. The aneka-janma-saṁsiddhi compound is preserved verbatim.
Adjacent — substrate-reattachment, but single-life single-discontinuity rather than many-births compounding.
Possible because 4.34 names knowledge-acquired-from-prostration, but the compounding-many-births structure is unique to 6.45.

verdict_payload.chapter_voice_summary: BG 6.45 closes the consolation arc in the chapter's senior-teacher register. The voice acknowledges the destination beyond engineering reach without flattening it. The eighteen-year staff-engineer scenario is grave and specific without slipping into hagiography. The 'sat with each failure across the years and refused to discharge the discomfort' phrasing is exactly the somatic register of the chapter's later verses. PASS.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: MEDIUM + STRETCHED. Multi-life metaphysics explicitly named as exceeding engineering. The kilbiṣa-purification is not mapped to literal sin-cleansing or chakra-clearing; it is mapped specifically to cognitive-bias-from-shipped-failures, which is a precise operational analog at strictly downscaled scope. Zero trivialization. PASS.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason_external: The verse names a destination past where any career reaches. The engineering layer explicitly preserves this. PASS.

verdict_payload.verdict_reason: The single disagreement entry on aneka-janma-saṁsiddhaḥ correctly distinguishes Shankara's saṁskāra-accumulation reading from Ramanuja's puṇya-accumulation reading. The 'verse's STRETCHED dimension for engineering analog' is explicitly named in the explanation. Score 8. PASS.

verdict_payload.reconstructed_sketch: Given chapter context (consolation arc closing toward 6.46), an engineering analog at position 45 would predictably address compounding-across-time. The specific kilbiṣa-as-cognitive-bias-from-shipped-failures mapping is verse-specific.
verdict_payload.verse_specific_signal: The 'striving-with-failure-encountered vs tenure-without-striving' dichotomy is 6.45's distinct contribution.

overlap_with_other_verses.explanation: Both in the consolation arc; 6.43 names substrate, 6.44 names current, 6.45 names destination. Sanctioned.

verdict_payload.reproducible_artifact: Promotion-committee rubric extension: 'Striving signal.' Three concrete prompts for the promo packet — (1) one architectural decision the candidate publicly rolled back; (2) one incident the candidate chaired with a structural post-mortem; (3) one cognitive bias the candidate identified in their own past work. Used in addition to scope/impact/leadership rubrics.
verdict_payload.embodiment_sketch: Lattice / Workday calibration template adds the striving-signal section. Candidate prepares; manager reviews; committee uses. Concretely copyable Monday.
